message:
I'll be honest—I'm usually the one calling my mom at 10 PM asking "did you remember to buy batteries?" So when the power went out last week, I was fully expecting to be fumbling around in the dark like always.

Except... I wasn't. I had a flashlight in the kitchen drawer (actually charged), a lantern in the hall closet, and a power bank I'd tossed in my bag weeks ago. My phone stayed alive. We had light. We didn't panic.

My kid asked if we could make it "camping night" indoors, and suddenly this minor inconvenience turned into kind of a fun thing. All because I'd finally gotten my act together.

Turns out being the *prepared* one feels pretty good. Who knew?

What's your blackout survival setup looking like right now—are you a "I've got this" person or more of a "we'll figure it out" type? 😊
